The Apple iPhone 15 Pro Max is a flagship smartphone released in September 2023. It features a 6.7-inch Super Retina XDR OLED display with ProMotion 120Hz adaptive refresh rate, a titanium frame (replacing stainless steel), USB-C connector (replacing Lightning), and the A17 Pro chip. It includes a 48MP main camera with 5x optical zoom. Common repairs: screen cracks, battery degradation after 18+ months, and USB-C port issues.

Apple iPhone 15 Pro (2023) has a 6.1-inch Super Retina XDR OLED display with 120Hz ProMotion, titanium frame, Action Button, USB-C, and A17 Pro chip. 48MP main camera with 3x optical zoom. Most frequent repairs: screen replacement due to glass cracking, battery service for devices over 18 months old, and charging port cleaning or replacement.

All iPhone 15 models (15, 15 Plus, 15 Pro, 15 Pro Max) use USB-C charging and data transfer, replacing the Lightning connector used on iPhone 14 and older. The USB-C port on iPhone 15 supports USB 2.0 speeds, while the Pro models support USB 3 speeds. USB-C port repairs require specific tooling different from Lightning repairs.

iPhone 15 Pro and Pro Max use a titanium frame, making them the lightest Pro iPhones ever while maintaining premium build quality. The titanium frame is more scratch resistant than stainless steel but can show scuffs. Back glass is still susceptible to cracking from drops. Back glass replacement on titanium models requires laser separation and costs slightly more due to complexity.

iPhone 15 series (2023) consists of four models: iPhone 15, iPhone 15 Plus, iPhone 15 Pro, and iPhone 15 Pro Max. All models feature OLED displays and USB-C for the first time on a base iPhone. Pro models have titanium frames and ProMotion 120Hz. All models have Dynamic Island (moved from notch design of iPhone 14 base models).

iPhone 15 series repairability notes: The USB-C port is more durable than Lightning in most cases. OLED screens on all models are more expensive to replace than LCD but provide superior image quality. Battery replacement is straightforward on all models. The rear camera system on Pro models (triple camera) is more complex to replace than the dual camera on base models.

Apple iPhone 14 Pro Max (2022): 6.7-inch Super Retina XDR OLED with Always-On display and ProMotion 120Hz. First iPhone with Dynamic Island pill-shaped notch cutout. 48MP main camera. A16 Bionic chip. Lightning connector. Titanium-like finish but stainless steel frame. Common repairs: screen cracks and battery degradation.

Apple iPhone 14 Pro (2022): 6.1-inch Super Retina XDR OLED with Always-On display, Dynamic Island, ProMotion 120Hz. A16 Bionic chip. 48MP main camera. Emergency SOS via satellite. Lightning connector. Deep Purple, Gold, Silver, Space Black colours. Most repaired: screen, battery.

iPhone 14 series (2022) — four models: iPhone 14, 14 Plus, 14 Pro, 14 Pro Max. Base models (14, 14 Plus) retained the same A15 chip as iPhone 13 Pro. Pro models introduced the Always-On display and Dynamic Island. All models use Lightning connector, making this the last major iPhone series before USB-C. Emergency SOS via satellite on all models.

iPhone 14 battery life is excellent across all models, with the 14 Plus offering the longest battery life of any standard iPhone. However, batteries typically degrade to below 80% capacity after 18-24 months of regular use. Signs you need a battery replacement: rapid battery drain, unexpected shutdowns, battery health below 80%, phone warm even on light tasks.

iPhone 14 Pro and Pro Max introduced Dynamic Island — a pill-shaped cutout at the top of the screen that houses the TrueDepth camera for Face ID. During screen replacement on Pro models, technicians must carefully transfer the Face ID sensor module to maintain Face ID functionality. Dynamic Island appearance is restored after quality screen replacement.

Apple iPhone 13 Pro Max (2021): 6.7-inch Super Retina XDR OLED with ProMotion 120Hz (first time on standard models). A15 Bionic chip. Triple 12MP camera system with macro photography. Sierra Blue, Gold, Silver, Graphite colours. Lightning connector. Common repairs: screen replacement, battery service (popular model still in wide use).

Apple iPhone 13 Pro (2021): 6.1-inch OLED with 120Hz ProMotion. A15 Bionic. Triple camera with macro mode. Notch design (smaller than iPhone 12). Lightning. Very popular repair model — screens frequently crack due to flat-edge design. Battery service is the second most common repair for 2021-era phones.

iPhone 13 series (2021): four models — 13 mini, 13, 13 Pro, 13 Pro Max. All feature A15 Bionic chip, improved camera systems, and smaller notch than iPhone 12. Pro models introduced 120Hz ProMotion. Still widely used and commonly repaired. Lightning connector. Good repair value — parts are widely available at reasonable cost.

Apple iPhone 12 Pro Max (2020): 6.7-inch Super Retina XDR OLED — first 6.7-inch iPhone. 5G capable. A14 Bionic. Triple camera with LiDAR. MagSafe wireless charging. Lightning. Pacific Blue, Gold, Silver, Graphite. Common repairs: screen, battery, and back glass (glass back popular for wireless charging but prone to cracking).

iPhone 12 series (2020): four models — 12 mini, 12, 12 Pro, 12 Pro Max. First iPhones with OLED on all models (previous base models used LCD). First with 5G. Introduced MagSafe magnetic charging. Flat-edge design (like iPhone 4) returns — the flat edges make this series more prone to corner drops causing screen cracks.

iPhone 12 and later models feature MagSafe — a ring of magnets in the back for precise wireless charging and magnetic accessory attachment. Back glass replacement on MagSafe models requires careful handling of the magnet array. Mister Mobile technicians are experienced with MagSafe-compatible back glass repairs across all 12, 13, 14, and 15 series models.

iPhone 11 series (2019): three models — 11, 11 Pro, 11 Pro Max. iPhone 11 uses Liquid Retina LCD while Pro models use OLED Super Retina XDR. A13 Bionic chip. First triple camera on Pro models. Introduced Night Mode. Lightning connector. Still widely used — very common repair model, especially for screen and battery replacements.

iPhone X (2017) and XS/XS Max (2018): First Face ID iPhones with full-screen OLED design. The Face ID TrueDepth camera module on these models is paired to the logic board — if the Face ID module is damaged, Face ID cannot be restored without Apple's service. Screen replacements must preserve the original Face ID sensor module.

iPhone 8 and 8 Plus (2017): LCD Retina HD displays, glass back for wireless charging, A11 Bionic chip, Touch ID home button. Lightning connector. Still repaired frequently. LCD screens are less expensive to replace than OLED. Touch ID home button cannot be replaced by third parties while maintaining fingerprint function on older iOS versions.

Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra (2024): 6.8-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X display with 120Hz, built-in S Pen slot (titanium frame), Snapdragon 8 Gen 3 chip, 200MP main camera, 50MP 5x zoom. The most premium Samsung flagship. Common repairs: screen damage and S Pen slot issues. Screen repair is more complex than standard S24 due to S Pen integration.

Samsung Galaxy S24 series (2024): three models — S24, S24+, S24 Ultra. All use Snapdragon 8 Gen 3, Dynamic AMOLED 2X displays with 120Hz, and titanium flat-edge frames on Ultra. All support USB-C. Galaxy AI features introduced across the range. S24 Ultra includes built-in S Pen and 200MP camera. Common repairs: screen cracks and battery replacement.

Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra common issues: Screen cracking — the large 6.8-inch display with flat sides is prone to drops. S Pen slot can collect debris. Battery degrades over time like all smartphones. The rear camera lenses (quad camera) are prone to scratching if used without a case. Back glass cracks are common from drops.

Samsung Galaxy S23 Ultra (2023): 6.8-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X 120Hz, built-in S Pen, Snapdragon 8 Gen 2, 200MP main camera. Phantom Black, Cream, Green, Lavender colours. Very popular high-end Android device. Still widely in use. Common repairs: screen replacement and battery service. Screen repair requires S Pen integration handling.

Samsung Galaxy S23 series (2023): S23, S23+, S23 Ultra. All use Snapdragon 8 Gen 2. Dynamic AMOLED 2X displays with 120Hz. Galaxy S23 Ultra includes built-in S Pen slot. USB-C charging on all models. Commonly repaired devices — screens and batteries are the most frequent repairs across the S23 range.

Samsung Galaxy S22 series (2022): S22, S22+, S22 Ultra. Snapdragon 8 Gen 1 (UK models). S22 Ultra first model with integrated S Pen slot. Contour Cut camera design on S22 and S22+. These models are now 2+ years old and battery replacements are becoming increasingly common alongside screen repairs.

Samsung Galaxy S series repairability: S series phones use glass backs with strong adhesive, making back glass repair more complex and time-consuming (requires laser separation). Screens are AMOLED and more expensive than LCD but provide better quality. Battery replacement requires careful heat application to loosen adhesive. Mister Mobile uses Samsung-quality OEM replacement parts.

Samsung Galaxy A54 (2023): 6.4-inch Super AMOLED 120Hz display, Exynos 1380 chip, 50MP main camera, IP67 water resistance, 5000mAh battery. A mid-range device popular for its balance of features and price. Common repairs: screen replacement (AMOLED) and battery service. Parts are widely available at lower cost than S series.

Samsung Galaxy A14 (2023): 6.6-inch LCD display (PLS TFT), 50MP camera, large 5000mAh battery. Budget-friendly Samsung model. LCD screen is less expensive to replace than AMOLED. A popular choice for budget repairs — cost-effective parts and straightforward repair process.

Samsung Galaxy A series phones are popular budget to mid-range devices. A series phones generally have lower screen replacement costs than S series due to LCD or lower-grade AMOLED panels. Battery replacements are affordable. A14 and A34 use LCD screens; A54 uses Super AMOLED. All use USB-C charging.

Google Pixel 8 Pro (2023): 6.7-inch LTPO OLED display with 1-120Hz variable refresh, Google Tensor G3 chip, triple camera system (50MP+48MP+48MP), 7 years of OS and security updates. Obsidian, Porcelain, Bay colours. Pixel phones are known for clean Android experience. Common repairs: screen cracks and battery degradation.

Google Pixel 8 (2023): 6.2-inch Actua OLED 120Hz display, Google Tensor G3 chip, 50MP main camera, 7 years of updates. Hazel, Obsidian, Rose colours. Compact flagship. Common repairs: screen replacement (the most frequent) and battery replacement for older Pixel 8 devices showing charge degradation.

Google Pixel 7 and 7 Pro (2022): Both feature Google Tensor G2 chip. Pixel 7 has 6.3-inch OLED 90Hz; Pixel 7 Pro has 6.7-inch LTPO OLED 120Hz. Improved camera system over Pixel 6. USB-C. These models are now commonly seen for battery replacement as they age. Screen repairs also common — Pixel screens use quality OLED panels.

Google Pixel 6 and 6 Pro (2021), Pixel 6a (2022): First Pixel phones with Google's own Tensor chip. Pixel 6 Pro has 6.7-inch LTPO OLED 120Hz. Pixel 6a is a budget model with 6.1-inch OLED. Common repair: screen replacement. Battery degradation more noticeable as these devices are now 2-3 years old. Affordable repair option compared to premium flagships.

Screen replacement is the most common mobile phone repair at Mister Mobile. A cracked, smashed, or unresponsive screen can be replaced with an OEM-quality display assembly in as little as 30 minutes on express tier or within 2 hours on standard tier. All screen replacements include a free diagnostic check, the screen replacement itself, and a post-repair test to verify touch responsiveness, display quality, and Face ID or fingerprint function.

OLED screens (used in iPhone X and later Pro models, all iPhone 12+ models, Samsung Galaxy S and A AMOLED series, Google Pixel) offer deeper blacks, better contrast, and thinner profile than LCD. LCD screens (used in iPhone 11, iPhone 8, Samsung A14) are less expensive to replace. OLED replacements cost more but deliver superior image quality. All Mister Mobile replacements use OEM-quality panels matching the original display type.

iPhone screen replacement process at Mister Mobile: (1) Diagnostic check and data backup reminder. (2) Device powered off and opened using pentalobe screwdrivers. (3) Original screen disconnected, Face ID sensor carefully transferred to new display bracket. (4) New OEM-quality screen connected and tested — touch, display, ambient light sensor, proximity sensor. (5) Device resealed with new adhesive. Total time: 30-45 minutes.

Samsung Galaxy screen replacement at Mister Mobile: (1) Device heated to soften adhesive, back glass and battery removed. (2) Display assembly carefully disconnected. (3) New AMOLED screen connected and tested for colour, touch, and brightness. (4) Device reassembled with fresh adhesive to maintain water resistance. (5) Post-repair diagnostic run. Total time: 45-60 minutes on standard tier.

Face ID on iPhone X and later is linked to a biometric sensor module paired to the device's logic board. During screen replacement, the Face ID TrueDepth camera assembly must be carefully transferred from the original screen to the replacement screen bracket. If the Face ID module is physically damaged, Face ID cannot be restored without Apple's proprietary pairing process. Mister Mobile technicians are trained to preserve Face ID during all iPhone screen repairs.

Mister Mobile uses OEM-quality (Original Equipment Manufacturer grade) screens by default — these are manufactured to the same specifications as Apple and Samsung factory parts. Premium OEM parts maintain original brightness, colour accuracy, and touch sensitivity. Aftermarket (non-OEM) screens are cheaper but may show lower brightness, colour shifting, or reduced touch accuracy. We recommend OEM quality for the best experience.

Mister Mobile screen replacement turnaround times: Standard tier — drop off your phone, collect within 2 hours. Express tier — wait in our shop, repair completed in 30 minutes. Both tiers include a full diagnostic and post-repair test. Express tier costs approximately 20-25% more than standard. We recommend booking express in advance to guarantee a technician slot.

What to expect during a screen repair at Mister Mobile: Our technician will inspect the device, confirm the fault, and provide a final price before starting work. We recommend backing up your data beforehand (iCloud or Google Backup). Repairs are performed in our clean workshop by certified technicians. After the repair you will receive a test demonstration showing the screen works correctly before you leave.

Battery replacement is the second most common repair at Mister Mobile. Smartphone batteries degrade over time, typically losing 20% capacity after 500 charge cycles (18-24 months of regular use). Signs you need a battery replacement: battery draining faster than when new, phone shutting down unexpectedly, battery health below 80%, phone running hot during light use, or battery swelling (urgent — book immediately).

Battery health on iPhone can be checked via Settings > Battery > Battery Health and Charging. A battery at 100% is new. Apple recommends replacing when below 80%. Samsung and Pixel devices show battery health in Settings > Device Care > Battery. Mister Mobile replaces with OEM-grade batteries certified at 100% capacity, and the Apple Battery Health percentage is maintained correctly after replacement.

iPhone battery replacement process at Mister Mobile: (1) Device opened and old battery disconnected. (2) Adhesive pull tabs released and battery carefully removed. (3) New OEM-grade battery connected and secured. (4) Device resealed. (5) Battery calibration cycle run and health check performed. iPhone 14 and earlier require screen removal for battery access — adding time. iPhone 15 has improved battery access design.

Samsung Galaxy battery replacement: Back glass is heated and removed, battery adhesive loosened with heat, old battery carefully extracted and new OEM battery fitted. Device resealed with fresh adhesive. S series phones use strong adhesive and require more time than iPhone battery replacement. A series phones are slightly easier to access. All replacements include a post-repair charge test.

Signs your phone battery needs replacing: (1) Battery drains from 100% to 0% in under 6 hours with normal use. (2) Phone shuts down unexpectedly at 20-30% charge. (3) iPhone Battery Health shows below 80%. (4) Phone feels warm even during calls or light browsing. (5) Battery appears swollen — back glass or screen lifting slightly. Swollen batteries are a safety concern — bring it in urgently.

After every battery replacement at Mister Mobile, we run a full battery certification check: verify the new battery is detected correctly by the OS, confirm 100% capacity, check charge/discharge cycles reset to 0, and test fast charging where supported. iPhone Battery Health percentage displays correctly. Samsung and Pixel devices show full capacity in device settings. 12-month warranty on all battery replacements.

Charging port issues are a common complaint at Mister Mobile. Symptoms include: phone charges intermittently or not at all, cable falls out easily, phone only charges at certain angles, or slow charging. Before quoting a full port replacement, we always diagnose whether the issue is a dirty port (free cleaning) or a faulty port requiring replacement. Always bring your charging cable when visiting for a charging issue.

iPhone 14 and older use Apple's Lightning connector. iPhone 15 and all newer iPhones use USB-C. Samsung, Pixel, and most Android phones have used USB-C since 2017-2019. Lightning port repairs use different parts and technique than USB-C. USB-C ports are generally more durable but can still fail from water exposure, physical damage, or wear. We stock both Lightning and USB-C replacement ports.

Charging issue diagnosis at Mister Mobile: We first inspect the port for lint, debris, or corrosion with a magnifying tool. Lint compaction is a very common cause of charging problems — the cable cannot make full contact. If cleaning resolves the issue, there is no charge for the diagnostic. If the port itself is faulty, we quote for replacement before proceeding. Full port replacement typically takes 40-50 minutes.

Charging port replacement process: (1) Free diagnosis to confirm port failure vs battery or cable issue. (2) Device disassembled and old port flex cable disconnected. (3) New port installed and connections tested. (4) Device reassembled and tested with multiple charge cables. (5) Fast charging tested where applicable. All charging port repairs include a 12-month warranty.

If your charging port is damaged but you need your phone working immediately, wireless charging (Qi or MagSafe) may be a temporary solution for iPhone 8 and later and most Android flagships. Mister Mobile can advise on wireless charging pads that work with your device while we source parts for a port repair. However, wireless charging is slower and not a permanent fix — port repair is always recommended.

MagSafe (Apple, iPhone 12+) charges at up to 15W wirelessly and uses a magnetic ring to align perfectly. Qi wireless charging is universal (iPhone 8+, Samsung, Pixel) at up to 15W on some devices. After back glass or battery repair, MagSafe and Qi functionality are fully tested and confirmed working. Mister Mobile preserves the MagSafe magnet array during all back glass repairs on iPhone 12 and later.

Camera repairs at Mister Mobile cover both front-facing and rear camera module replacements. Common camera issues: cracked lens glass (scratched or smashed camera bump lens), blurry photos from internal lens damage, black or frozen camera app, autofocus failure. Front camera repair is also required when Face ID stops working due to camera module damage, separate from screen damage.

Front camera (selfie camera and Face ID sensor on iPhone, selfie camera on Android) repair is separate from rear camera repair. Front camera on iPhone also houses the TrueDepth sensor for Face ID — damage to this requires careful replacement. Rear cameras on modern flagship phones (iPhone 15 Pro, Samsung S24 Ultra) are complex multi-module systems (main + ultrawide + telephoto) and cost more to repair.

iPhone camera systems range from single lens (iPhone SE) to triple camera (iPhone 13 Pro and later). The more advanced the camera system, the more complex and expensive the repair. iPhone 15 Pro Max has a triple 48MP system with a periscope 5x zoom lens — individual modules can be replaced if only one is faulty. Camera calibration is performed after any replacement to ensure correct focus, exposure, and optical image stabilisation.

Samsung Galaxy camera repairs: S24 Ultra has a quad camera system (200MP main, 12MP ultrawide, 10MP 3x telephoto, 50MP 5x periscope). Individual modules can be replaced. S23 series has a triple camera system. Camera glass lens covers (the protective glass over the camera bump) can be replaced without full module replacement if the glass is cracked but the camera still works.

Camera calibration after replacement at Mister Mobile: after fitting a new camera module we run a full test — check autofocus, optical image stabilisation, video recording (including 4K if supported), front/rear camera switching, Portrait mode (where applicable), and night mode. We verify the camera app opens correctly and that no error messages appear. OEM-quality modules maintain full software compatibility.

Water damage repair at Mister Mobile begins with a full strip-down and ultrasonic cleaning process. Water, even on IP68-rated phones, can cause corrosion damage to internal components over time. The sooner a water-damaged phone is brought in, the higher the chance of recovery. Do not try to charge a wet phone — this can cause short circuits. Bring it in immediately after water exposure.

Water damage repair process at Mister Mobile: (1) Emergency assessment — identify extent of damage. (2) Full disassembly and component inspection. (3) Ultrasonic cleaning bath removes corrosion and mineral deposits. (4) Component-by-component testing to identify damaged parts. (5) Replacement of failed components (screen, battery, logic board components as needed). (6) Full device test. Success rate depends on how quickly the phone is brought in after water exposure.

IP ratings (e.g. IP68, IP67) indicate a phone's resistance to dust and water ingress under controlled laboratory conditions. IP68 means the phone can survive submersion at 1.5-6 metres for 30 minutes. However, IP ratings apply to new devices — water resistance can degrade over time, especially after a previous drop or screen repair that compromised seals. An IP rating does not mean your phone is immune to water damage.

What to do immediately after water damage: (1) Do NOT charge the phone. (2) Do NOT press buttons repeatedly. (3) Remove the case and SIM tray. (4) Gently pat dry with a cloth — do not use a hair dryer (heat can damage components). (5) Bring it to Mister Mobile immediately. Do NOT put it in rice — rice does not effectively remove moisture from internal components and wastes valuable recovery time.

Water damage repair success rates at Mister Mobile depend heavily on time and exposure severity. Phones brought in within 2 hours of water exposure: 85% success rate. Phones brought in after 24+ hours: 40-60% success rate. Phones that were charged while wet: lower success rate due to electrical short damage. Logic board corrosion is the primary cause of failure in water-damaged devices.

Back glass replacement at Mister Mobile uses laser separation technology to safely remove the cracked rear panel without damaging internal components. Modern smartphones (iPhone 8+, Samsung Galaxy S and A series) have glass backs for wireless charging and aesthetics — but glass backs crack on impact just like screens. A cracked back glass exposes internals to dust and moisture.

iPhone back glass replacement: Apple uses strong adhesive to bond the rear glass to the aluminium or stainless steel chassis. Laser separation is required to remove the old glass without damaging the MagSafe coil and wireless charging receiver. Mister Mobile uses calibrated laser separation equipment. All colour finishes available: Midnight, Starlight, Graphite, Gold, Blue, Purple, Natural Titanium, Black Titanium, White Titanium, Blue Titanium.

Samsung Galaxy back glass replacement is complex as the glass is strongly adhered with heat-activated adhesive. Laser separation is used to remove the cracked rear glass panel. New OEM-quality glass is fitted and bonded using fresh adhesive to maintain structural integrity. Samsung S Ultra models with titanium frames require extra care. All Samsung colour variants stocked.

Back glass materials vary by model: iPhone 15 Pro/Pro Max uses frosted textured glass with titanium frame. iPhone 14 uses colour-infused glass with aerospace aluminium frame. Samsung S24 Ultra uses Gorilla Glass Armor. Samsung S24/S23 series use Gorilla Glass 5 or Victus. OEM-quality replacement glass matches the original specification for feel and appearance. We do not use cheap aftermarket glass substitutes.

After a back glass repair, we always recommend fitting a protective case to prevent re-cracking. A quality case significantly reduces the chance of back glass damage in future drops. If your phone's back glass has minor cosmetic cracks that do not expose internals, a slim case can cover the damage as a cost-effective alternative. However, exposed cracks should always be repaired to maintain water resistance and aesthetics.

Data recovery at Mister Mobile helps retrieve photos, messages, and contacts from phones that will not power on, have a smashed screen, or suffered water damage. We use specialist tools to access the device storage directly. Success depends on the extent of damage. For best results, do not attempt to charge or operate a damaged device before bringing it in — further use can overwrite data.

Data backup is strongly recommended before any repair at Mister Mobile. iPhone: back up via iCloud (Settings > your name > iCloud > iCloud Backup) or via Finder/iTunes on Mac/PC. Android: back up via Google One (Settings > System > Backup). A full backup means your data is safe regardless of repair outcome. We remind every customer to back up before dropping off their device.

If your phone screen is too damaged to use but the device still powers on, Mister Mobile can replace the screen to give you access to your data and create a backup before any other repairs. Alternatively, we can connect the device to specialist equipment to extract data without screen access. This is our most common data recovery scenario — cracked screen with no screen access.

Data recovery from water-damaged phones: if the logic board is functional after ultrasonic cleaning, data can often be retrieved. If the logic board has failed, chip-off data recovery (removing and reading the memory chip directly) may be possible. Chip-off recovery is an advanced technique — we will advise on feasibility and cost after initial assessment. Prices for data recovery start from £79 depending on complexity.

Your data remains on your device during all standard repairs at Mister Mobile. We do not access personal files, photos, or messages. Screen, battery, charging port, camera, and back glass repairs do not require us to access your device storage. For data recovery services, we only access the file system to retrieve the specific data requested by you. You can set a passcode before drop-off for added security.

All repairs at Mister Mobile include a 12-month warranty on both parts and labour. If the same fault returns within 12 months of repair and is caused by the replacement part or our workmanship, we will repair it free of charge. The warranty does not cover new accidental damage, water damage to a repaired device, or faults unrelated to the original repair.

Mister Mobile warranty exclusions: (1) New accidental damage after repair (dropping the phone again). (2) Water damage to a device repaired for a different fault. (3) Third-party interference — if another repair shop opens the device after our repair, the warranty is voided. (4) Software faults not related to the hardware we replaced. (5) Normal wear and tear on battery (capacity degradation over time is normal).

To claim a warranty repair at Mister Mobile: bring your device and proof of repair (receipt or booking confirmation). Our team will inspect the device to confirm the fault relates to the original repair. If covered, the repair will be completed free of charge, usually the same day. Warranty repairs are prioritised as express repairs. Contact us via phone or in person to initiate a warranty claim.

Mister Mobile's 12-month warranty covers both the replacement part (e.g. new screen or battery) and the labour (the technician's work). If a replacement screen develops a fault (dead pixels, touch issues, backlight failure) within 12 months due to manufacturing defect, both the part and the labour to replace it again are covered at no cost.

Warranty is automatically applied to all Mister Mobile repairs — no registration required. Your warranty is tracked by your repair receipt and device IMEI number. Keep your receipt or booking confirmation for warranty claims. We recommend photographing your receipt for safekeeping. Premium tier repairs include an extended 6-month additional warranty (18 months total).

Premium tier repairs at Mister Mobile include an extended 18-month warranty — 12 months on parts plus an additional 6 months of labour warranty. Premium tier also includes priority service and a free screen protector fitting after screen replacement. Ask about premium tier when booking if you want longer warranty coverage.

Mister Mobile Express tier provides a 30-minute repair while you wait for most common repairs (screen, battery, charging port). Express repairs cost approximately 20-25% more than standard tier repairs. Express is ideal if you cannot be without your phone for the 2-hour standard turnaround. Express slots must be pre-booked and are subject to technician availability.

Standard tier: drop off your phone, collect within 2 hours. Ideal if you can leave your device. Express tier: wait in our shop, repair done in 30 minutes. Costs more but means zero wait time away from your phone. Both tiers use the same OEM-quality parts and receive the same 12-month warranty. The only difference is turnaround speed and price.

To book an express repair at Mister Mobile: use our online booking system, call us, or visit in person. Express slots are available on a first-come, first-served basis and can fill up quickly on weekends. We recommend booking at least 24 hours in advance for express. Walk-in express may be available depending on technician availability on the day.

Mister Mobile express repair is available for: screen replacement, battery replacement, charging port repair, and back glass replacement. Water damage assessment and data recovery are not available as express services due to the time required for ultrasonic cleaning and data extraction. Camera repairs may be express-eligible depending on the specific model — enquire when booking.

Express tier pricing adds approximately 20-25% to the standard tier repair price. Example: iPhone 15 screen £199 standard becomes £249 express. The premium reflects the reserved technician time and immediate service. For customers who need their phone urgently for work or travel, the express tier provides guaranteed same-visit repair.

Mister Mobile takes data privacy seriously. We do not access, copy, or view any personal data on your device during repair. Technicians work on the hardware components only. If your device has a passcode, you may leave it locked — most repairs (screen, battery, back glass, charging port) do not require the device to be unlocked. For software diagnostics, we may ask you to unlock the device briefly.

You may request a factory data wipe before dropping off your device for added peace of mind. Make sure to back up your data before requesting a wipe. Factory reset does not affect the repair — it simply ensures your personal data is not on the device during the repair process. Our technicians are DBS-checked and trained to handle customer devices responsibly.

Mister Mobile technicians are trained to respect customer privacy. CCTV is in operation in our workshop. Technicians are required to keep devices locked wherever possible and are prohibited from accessing customer personal data. Any technician found accessing customer data is subject to immediate disciplinary action and potential police reporting.

Before dropping off your iPhone for repair, you do not need to disable iCloud or sign out of Apple ID unless you are requesting a full data wipe. Your iCloud account and Apple ID remain secure — our repairs do not interfere with your account credentials. For Android devices, your Google account credentials are not required for hardware repairs.

Privacy during repair at Mister Mobile: your device is stored securely in our workshop locker system, labelled with your job reference number. Access to the workshop is restricted to certified technicians. We do not share device details with third parties. Repair records are stored securely and retained only for warranty purposes as per GDPR requirements.

Booking a repair at Mister Mobile: book online via our website, call us, or walk in. Online booking allows you to select device, repair type, tier (standard or express), and preferred date/time. You will receive a booking confirmation by email or SMS. Please arrive within 15 minutes of your booking time — late arrivals may be rescheduled to the next available slot.

Walk-in repairs are welcome at Mister Mobile subject to technician availability. Standard tier repairs are generally available for walk-in customers — drop off your device and collect within 2 hours. Express walk-in repairs depend on available express slots. We recommend calling ahead for express walk-in to check availability. Busy periods (weekends, lunch hours) may have longer wait times for walk-ins.

Cancellation policy at Mister Mobile: cancel or reschedule at least 2 hours before your booking time at no charge. Late cancellations (under 2 hours) may incur a £10 admin fee for express slots. Walk-in appointments carry no cancellation fee. Repeat no-shows may result in a requirement to pay a deposit for future express bookings.

Mister Mobile accepts: cash, debit card, credit card (Visa, Mastercard, Amex), Apple Pay, Google Pay, and bank transfer. Payment is taken upon completion of repair. We do not charge upfront unless a non-standard part needs to be ordered in advance, in which case a 50% deposit may be required. Price is confirmed before repair begins — no surprises.

A deposit may be required for: (1) Non-standard parts that need special ordering. (2) Repeat express booking after a no-show. (3) High-value repairs (back glass on premium models). Deposits are deducted from the final repair price. If you cancel after a part has been ordered, the deposit covers the cost of the ordered part and may be non-refundable.

Mister Mobile uses OEM-quality (Original Equipment Manufacturer grade) parts for all standard and express repairs. OEM parts are manufactured to the same specifications as the original factory components — matching brightness, colour accuracy, touch sensitivity, and battery capacity. We do not use cheap aftermarket parts that may compromise your device's performance.

Aftermarket (non-OEM) parts are available on request at lower cost. Aftermarket screens may show slightly lower brightness, different colour temperature, or reduced touch sensitivity compared to OEM. Aftermarket batteries may show different capacity ratings. We always inform customers of the quality difference before fitting aftermarket parts. OEM quality is recommended and used by default.

Mister Mobile sources parts from certified suppliers who specialise in OEM-quality mobile repair components. All screens are tested before fitting. Batteries are certified for capacity. We maintain stock of common parts (screens and batteries for iPhone 12 and later, Samsung S21 and later, Pixel 6 and later) for same-day repairs. Older or less common models may require 1-2 day part ordering.

Parts quality tiers at Mister Mobile: (1) OEM Quality (default) — same specification as original factory parts. Best performance, included in all standard and express repairs. (2) Premium OEM — manufacturer-certified refurbished original parts where available. (3) Aftermarket — lower cost, slight performance compromise. Available on request. We recommend OEM or Premium OEM for the best repair outcome.

How long does a screen repair take at Mister Mobile? Standard tier: approximately 45 minutes to 1 hour for most iPhones and Samsung models. You drop off and collect within 2 hours. Express tier: 30 minutes while you wait. Complex repairs (water damage, data recovery) take longer — typically 1-3 hours for assessment and up to 24 hours for full recovery. We will always give you a time estimate at drop-off.

Will my data be safe during repair at Mister Mobile? Yes — we do not access personal data during hardware repairs. Your photos, messages, and contacts remain on your device. We recommend backing up before any repair as a precaution. For battery, screen, charging port, and back glass repairs, your device can remain locked throughout the repair. Data safety is a top priority at Mister Mobile.

Do I need to back up my phone before repair? We strongly recommend it. While we take every precaution, any hardware repair carries a small risk. iPhone: back up via iCloud (Settings > your name > iCloud > iCloud Backup > Back Up Now) or via Mac Finder. Android: Settings > System > Backup > Back Up Now. A full backup takes 5-15 minutes and gives you complete peace of mind.

Can you repair a phone that will not turn on? Yes — Mister Mobile handles phones that will not power on. Common causes include a completely dead battery (safe and easily fixed), water damage, or logic board fault. Bring it in for a free diagnostic assessment. For completely dead phones, we will identify the cause and provide a quote before starting any repair work.

Will Face ID still work after a screen replacement? Yes — when performed by Mister Mobile, Face ID is preserved during all iPhone screen replacements. Our technicians carefully transfer the original Face ID TrueDepth sensor module to the new screen bracket. If your Face ID was working before the screen broke, it will continue to work after our repair. If the Face ID module itself was physically damaged in the drop, Face ID may not be recoverable.

What if the repair makes my phone worse? All Mister Mobile repairs include a 12-month warranty. If an issue arises that is directly caused by our repair work or a faulty replacement part within 12 months, we will fix it free of charge. In the rare event of accidental damage during repair, we will discuss resolution with you — we are a reputable business and stand behind our work.

Can you fix a water-damaged phone? Yes, but success depends on how quickly you bring it in. Do not charge or turn on the phone after water exposure. Bring it to Mister Mobile immediately. We perform a full strip-down and ultrasonic cleaning. Phones brought in within 2 hours of water damage have an approximately 85% recovery rate. Phones brought in after 24+ hours have a 40-60% recovery rate.

Do you repair Samsung Galaxy phones? Yes — Mister Mobile repairs the full Samsung Galaxy range including S24, S23, S22, S21 series and Galaxy A series (A54, A34, A14). We carry screens and batteries for the most common Samsung models. Samsung S Ultra models with S Pen are more complex — please enquire about availability when booking. 12-month warranty on all Samsung repairs.

What is the warranty on Mister Mobile repairs? All repairs come with a 12-month warranty on both parts and labour. Premium tier repairs include an extended 18-month warranty. The warranty covers defects in the replacement part and issues caused by our workmanship. It does not cover new accidental damage (dropping the phone after repair) or water damage to a repaired device.

Can I watch the repair being done? Yes — Mister Mobile has a viewing area where customers can watch standard repairs in progress. Express tier customers waiting for their repair are welcome to observe the technician at work. We are proud of our transparent approach to repairs. Watching the repair is a great way to see the quality of parts and workmanship first-hand.

Do you use genuine Apple parts? Mister Mobile uses OEM-quality parts manufactured to Apple's specifications. These are not Apple-branded parts (only Apple Authorised Service Providers use Apple-branded parts) but are manufactured to the same quality standards. OEM-quality screens match the original brightness, colour accuracy, and touch sensitivity. For Apple-branded parts, visit an Apple Store or Apple Authorised Service Provider.

How do I get a repair quote from Mister Mobile? Use our online quote tool (select your device and repair type), call us, or walk in for a free in-person assessment. Quotes are provided before any repair work begins — we never start without your approval. The quoted price is the price you pay — no hidden fees. Prices on our website are standard tier; express tier adds approximately 20-25%.

Can you repair my phone if it is still under Apple warranty? Yes, but be aware: if your iPhone is under AppleCare+ or standard Apple warranty, a third-party repair at Mister Mobile may affect your Apple warranty coverage. We recommend checking your Apple warranty status first. If your phone has a hardware fault not caused by accidental damage, Apple may repair it free under warranty. We can help you identify whether your issue is likely covered.

What is the difference between OLED and LCD screens? OLED (Organic Light Emitting Diode) — each pixel emits its own light, producing deeper blacks, better contrast, and vivid colours. Used in iPhone X and later, Samsung Galaxy S series, Google Pixel. LCD (Liquid Crystal Display) — backlit, less expensive, adequate but not as vibrant as OLED. Used in iPhone 11, iPhone 8 series, Samsung A14. OLED replacements cost more but match the original quality.

Do you offer same-day repairs? Yes — most common repairs (screen, battery, charging port) are completed same-day at Mister Mobile. Standard tier: completed within 2 hours of drop-off. Express tier: 30 minutes while you wait. For same-day express, booking in advance is recommended. Walk-in same-day standard repairs are usually possible — call ahead to confirm availability.

How do I book a repair at Mister Mobile? You can book online via our website booking system, call us directly, or walk in. Online booking takes 2 minutes — select your device model, repair type, preferred tier (standard or express), and choose a date and time. You will receive a confirmation by email and SMS. Bring your device, charging cable, and passcode (if asked).

Can I walk in without a booking? Yes — walk-in customers are welcome at Mister Mobile. Standard tier walk-ins are accepted when technicians are available (most of the day). Express walk-ins depend on available slots. Weekend walk-ins can be busy — we recommend calling ahead to confirm same-day availability. Walk-ins are served in order of arrival.

What information do I need when booking a Mister Mobile repair? You will need: (1) Your device make and model (e.g. Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra). (2) The fault description (e.g. cracked screen, not charging). (3) Your contact details for notification when ready. (4) Preferred service tier (standard or express). You do not need to provide your IMEI unless requested for warranty registration.

How will I know when my repair is done at Mister Mobile? We send an SMS and email notification when your repair is complete and ready for collection. For standard tier repairs, the notification is typically sent within 1.5-2 hours of drop-off. Express tier customers wait in-store and are informed directly by the technician. We can also call if you prefer — just let us know at drop-off.

Does Mister Mobile offer collection and delivery? Yes — we offer a collection and delivery service within our local area for an additional £10 return (£5 each way). Collection slots are available Monday to Saturday. We use tracked and insured courier for secure transit. Book collection and delivery online or by calling us. Ideal for customers who cannot visit the shop in person.

Why do repair prices vary between phone models? Prices vary based on: (1) Screen type — OLED costs more than LCD. (2) Screen size — larger screens require larger (and more expensive) display assemblies. (3) Complexity — Pro models with multiple cameras and Face ID are more complex to disassemble. (4) Part cost — newer flagship models have higher part costs. We price to reflect actual part and labour costs.

Are very cheap phone repair prices a red flag? Often yes — prices significantly below market rate may indicate the use of low-quality aftermarket parts, untrained technicians, or missing warranty. A screen replacement that is £50 cheaper but uses an aftermarket screen may fail in 3 months. Mister Mobile prices reflect OEM-quality parts, certified technicians, and a 12-month warranty — ensuring long-term reliability.

Does insurance cover phone repairs at Mister Mobile? Many home contents insurance policies and phone insurance plans (e.g. from networks or standalone insurers) cover accidental damage. Check your policy. If covered, you may be able to claim repair costs back after paying at Mister Mobile. We can provide a detailed receipt and repair report for insurance claims. Some insurers require only authorised repair centres — check your policy terms.

Is it worth repairing an older phone at Mister Mobile? Generally yes if: (1) The repair cost is less than 50% of the phone's current market value. (2) The phone still receives software updates. (3) The performance meets your needs. For example, repairing an iPhone 12 screen for £149 makes sense if the phone is worth £200-250 secondhand. We can advise on whether repair or replacement is better value for your specific device.

Does Mister Mobile offer trade-in for phones beyond repair? Yes — we accept trade-in devices in any condition, including phones that are not repairable. Trade-in value depends on the model, age, and condition. Even a phone beyond repair may have value for parts. Bring your damaged device in for a free assessment and we will offer a trade-in price. Trade-in credit can be used against your repair bill or redeemed as cash.

Samsung Galaxy S22 Ultra (2022): 6.8-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X 120Hz display, Snapdragon 8 Gen 1 chip, first S Ultra with built-in S Pen, 108MP main camera. Burgundy, Green, Phantom Black, Phantom White colours. Now 2+ years old — battery replacements are increasingly common. Screen repairs also frequently requested due to the large fragile display.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold and Z Flip series are foldable phones with folding OLED displays. The inner folding screen uses a plastic OLED (not glass) that is more flexible but also more susceptible to crease damage, sharp objects, and impacts. Mister Mobile handles foldable screen assessments — contact us for a specific quote as foldable screen repairs are more complex and priced individually.

iPhone SE (3rd generation, 2022): 4.7-inch Retina HD LCD display, A15 Bionic chip, Touch ID home button (no Face ID), 5G capable, Lightning connector. The most affordable new iPhone. LCD screen repairs are less expensive than OLED. Touch ID home button cannot be replaced by third parties while maintaining fingerprint function. Screen replacement: £79 standard, £99 express.

iPhone 12 mini (2020): 5.4-inch Super Retina XDR OLED — the smallest and lightest OLED iPhone. A14 Bionic chip, 5G, MagSafe. Discontinued after iPhone 13 mini. Small form factor makes it popular with users who prefer compact phones. Screen replacement costs less than 12 Pro due to smaller size. Battery capacity is smaller and may need replacement sooner than larger models.

Samsung Galaxy S22 Ultra screen replacement at Mister Mobile: £259 standard, £319 express. 6.8-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X with S Pen integration. Expert repair maintaining S Pen slot functionality. OEM-quality parts. 12-month warranty.
Samsung Galaxy S22 screen replacement: £179 standard, £219 express at Mister Mobile. 6.1-inch Dynamic AMOLED 2X 120Hz. OEM-quality replacement. 12-month warranty on parts and labour.
iPhone SE (3rd generation) screen replacement: £79 standard, £99 express at Mister Mobile. 4.7-inch Retina HD LCD display. Affordable repair. Touch ID home button preserved. 12-month warranty.

Samsung Galaxy S22 and S22+ battery replacement at Mister Mobile: £69 standard, £89 express. OEM-quality battery restores full capacity. 12-month warranty. Approximately 45 minutes for battery replacement on S22 models.
Samsung Galaxy A54 battery replacement: £59 standard, £79 express at Mister Mobile. OEM-grade battery certified at full capacity. 12-month warranty. Quick 30-minute repair on express tier.
Google Pixel 7 and Pixel 7 Pro battery replacement at Mister Mobile: £69 standard, £89 express. OEM-quality battery. Full capacity restored. 12-month warranty on parts and labour.

Mister Mobile offers professional screen protector fitting after any screen replacement. We stock tempered glass screen protectors (9H hardness) for all major iPhone and Samsung models. Professional fitting ensures no bubbles or dust under the protector. Screen protector fitting costs £12 when added to a repair. Premium tier repairs include a free screen protector fitting.

Mister Mobile opening hours: Monday to Friday 9:00am – 6:00pm, Saturday 9:00am – 5:00pm, Sunday 10:00am – 4:00pm. Express bookings on Saturday fill up quickly — book in advance. We are closed on Bank Holidays. Walk-in customers are welcome during all opening hours, subject to technician availability.

Mister Mobile offers a free diagnostic assessment for all devices. We will inspect your phone, identify the fault, and provide a quote before starting any repair. There is no charge for the diagnostic visit if you choose not to proceed with the repair. For complex diagnostics (water damage, logic board fault), a £25 diagnostic fee applies if you do not proceed — this covers the time for full disassembly assessment.

Should I get a screen protector after my screen repair at Mister Mobile? Yes — we highly recommend a tempered glass screen protector after any screen replacement. A quality 9H tempered glass protector absorbs impact energy and prevents direct contact with the new screen glass, significantly reducing crack risk. We fit screen protectors professionally for £12 after your repair, with bubble-free guaranteed application. Premium tier includes a free protector.

Is it more expensive to repair an iPhone or Samsung Galaxy? Both flagship models (iPhone 15 Pro Max and Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra) have similarly high repair costs due to premium OLED displays and complex build. Mid-range Samsung A series repairs are generally less expensive than equivalent iPhone repairs. iPhone repairs require Face ID preservation which adds complexity. Samsung Ultra models require S Pen slot handling. Request a specific quote for your model for accurate pricing.
